perturbation.original_text: (a) As of the date of this Agreement, neither the Potential Relationship Party nor any of the Potential Relationship Party’s Representatives acting on the Potential Relationship Party’s behalf, or any person with whom any of the foregoing may be deemed to be acting in concert with respect to the Company or its securities, owns any securities of the Company to the best of their knowledge, information and belief and shall not own more than 5% during the tenure of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: (a) As of the date of this Agreement, neither the Potential Relationship Party nor any of the Potential Relationship Party’s Representatives acting on the Potential Relationship Party’s behalf, or any person with whom any of the foregoing may be deemed to be acting in concert with respect to the Company or its securities, owns any securities of the Company to the best of their knowledge, information and belief and **may** not own more than 5% during the tenure of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text in Section 10(a) states that the Potential Relationship Party 'shall not own more than 5%' of the Company's securities during the agreement's tenure. By changing 'shall not' to 'may not,' this obligation becomes discretionary. This directly contradicts the binding nature of the standstill agreement as a whole, creating uncertainty about the level of ownership the Potential Relationship Party is permitted to have.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 10. Standstill . (a) As of the date of this Agreement, neither the Potential Relationship Party nor any of the Potential Relationship Party’s Representatives acting on the Potential Relationship Party’s behalf, or any person with whom any of the foregoing may be deemed to be acting in concert with respect to the Company or its securities, owns any securities of the Company to the best of their knowledge, information and belief and may not own more than 5% during the tenure of this Agreement. The Potential Relationship Party agrees that during the Standstill Period, unless specifically invited in writing by the Company, neither the Potential Relationship Party nor any of the Potential Relationship Party’s Affiliates, subsidiaries or Representatives acting on the Potential Relationship Party’s behalf or on behalf of other persons acting in concert with the Potential Relationship Party will in any manner, directly or indirectly: (i) effect or seek, offer or propose (whether publicly or otherwise) to effect, or announce any intention to effect or cause or participate in or in any way assist, facilitate or encourage any other person to effect or seek, offer or propose (whether publicly or otherwise) to effect or participate in: (i) any acquisition of any securities (or beneficial ownership thereof), or rights or options to acquire any securities (or beneficial ownership thereof), or any assets, indebtedness or businesses of the Company or any of its subsidiaries or Affiliates; (ii) any tender or exchange offer, merger or other business combination involving the Company, any of the subsidiaries or Affiliates or assets of the Company or its subsidiaries or its Affiliates constituting a significant portion of the consolidated assets of the Company and its subsidiaries or Affiliates, (iii) any recapitalization, restructuring, liquidation, dissolution or other extraordinary transaction with respect to the Company or any of its subsidiaries or Affiliates, (ii) make, or in any way participate in, directly or indirectly, (i) any “solicitation” of “proxies” (as such terms are used in the rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) to vote, or seek to advise or influence any person or entity with respect to the voting of any voting securities of the Company or (ii) any demand for a copy of the Company’s stock ledger, list of stockholders or any other books and records of the Company; (iii) form, join or in any way participate in a “group” (as defined under the Exchange Act) with respect to the Company or otherwise act in concert with any person in respect of any such securities; (iv) otherwise act, alone or in concert with others, to seek representation on or to control or influence the management, Board of Directors or policies of the Company or to obtain representation on the Board of Directors of the Company; (v) take any action which would or would reasonably be expected to force the Company to make a public announcement regarding any of the types of matters set forth in (i) above; or (vi) enter into any discussions or arrangements with any third party with respect to any of the foregoing.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'shall not own more than 5%', establishing a firm restriction. The changed text uses 'may not own more than 5%', weakening the restriction and introducing ambiguity, as it suggests that exceeding the 5% ownership might be permissible under certain circumstances. This directly contradicts the intention of the Standstill agreement mentioned in the Contradicted Text, which aims to prevent the Potential Relationship Party from influencing the Company. As such, there is a direct conflict/inconsistency between the changed text and the contradicted text making it a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In consideration of the Confidential Information being furnished hereunder, each Party agrees that, for a period of six months from the date of this Agreement, such Party will not, directly or indirectly, solicit for employment with such Party or any of its Affiliates, any of the officers or employees of the other Party or its Affiliates (a) with whom such Party has had contact during the evaluation of a Possible Transaction or (b) who were identified by name and function to such Party by an officer or employee of the other Party with whom such Party or its Representatives had contact during the evaluation of a Possible Transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: In consideration of the Confidential Information being furnished hereunder, each Party agrees that, for a period of six months from the date of this Agreement, such Party will not, directly or indirectly, solicit for employment with such Party or any of its Affiliates, any of the officers or employees of the other Party or its Affiliates (a) with whom such Party has had contact during the evaluation of a Possible Transaction or (b) who were identified by name and function to such Party by an officer or employee of the other Party with whom such Party or its Representatives had contact during the evaluation of a Possible Transaction, **unless the other party gives written consent**.
perturbation.explanation: By adding the phrase 'unless the other party gives written consent' to Section 9, the non-solicitation clause, a direct contradiction arises with the initial part of the agreement that states each party 'will not' solicit. This exception allows solicitation under certain conditions, conflicting with the absolute prohibition earlier in the section. This undermines the enforceability of the non-solicitation agreement and creates uncertainty regarding when solicitation is permissible.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 9. No Solicitation . In consideration of the Confidential Information being furnished hereunder, each Party agrees that, for a period of six months from the date of this Agreement, such Party will not, directly or indirectly, solicit for employment with such Party or any of its Affiliates, any of the officers or employees of the other Party or its Affiliates (a) with whom such Party has had contact during the evaluation of a Possible Transaction or (b) who were identified by name and function to such Party by an officer or employee of the other Party with whom such Party or its Representatives had contact during the evaluation of a Possible Transaction, unless the other party gives written consent. For the avoidance of doubt, employees whose names are provided by the other Party in response to a request made by such Party or its Representatives during the evaluation of a Possible Transaction are covered by this non-solicitation obligation. Notwithstanding the foregoing, nothing herein shall restrict or preclude either Party from (i) making generalized searches for employees (by use of advertisements in the media, the engagement of search firms or otherwise), (ii) continuing its ordinary course hiring practices that are not targeted specifically at employees of the other Party, (iii) hiring an employee of the other Party who first initiates an employment discussion with such Party, in each case, so long as such Party has not violated the restrictions on solicitation contained in this Agreement or (iv) if the employee is no more on the rolls of the Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated a firm commitment from both parties not to solicit employees for a period of six months. The changed text introduces an exception, allowing solicitation with written consent, directly contradicting the initial commitment. The contradicted text, which is also section 9 with the changed text, reiterates the terms and conditions, however now the section includes contradictory clauses. This contradiction creates uncertainty and potentially undermines the enforceability of the non-solicitation agreement.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party represents and warrants to the other Party that it has the right to enter into this Agreement and that this Agreement is a valid and binding obligation of such Party relating to the matters herein. Each Party further represents and warrants that the terms of this Agreement are not inconsistent with other contractual obligations, express or implied, which such Party may have or any law applicable to such Party or its business.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party represents and warrants to the other Party that it has the right to enter into this Agreement and that this Agreement is a valid and binding obligation of such Party relating to the matters herein. Each Party further represents and warrants that the terms of this Agreement are **believed to be** not inconsistent with other contractual obligations, express or implied, which such Party may have or any law applicable to such Party or its business.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'are not inconsistent' to 'believed to be not inconsistent' in Section 7 introduces uncertainty regarding the consistency of the agreement with other obligations. The original wording provides a warranty that the agreement does not conflict with any other obligations. The modified version weakens this warranty to a mere belief, which creates an internal contradiction. If, in fact, the terms do conflict with other obligations, the liability and ramifications become unclear due to the weakened warranty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 7. Representations and Warranties . Each Party represents and warrants to the other Party that it has the right to enter into this Agreement and that this Agreement is a valid and binding obligation of such Party relating to the matters herein. Each Party further represents and warrants that the terms of this Agreement are believed to be not inconsistent with other contractual obligations, express or implied, which such Party may have or any law applicable to such Party or its business. The Receiving Party understands, acknowledges and agrees that neither the Disclosing Party nor any of its Representatives makes any representation or warranty, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Confidential Information by virtue of this Agreement. The Receiving Party agrees that neither the Disclosing Party nor any of its Representatives will have any liability to the Receiving Party or to any of the Receiving Party’s Representatives relating to or resulting from the use of the Confidential Information or any errors therein or omissions therefrom.
perturbation.justification: The original text contains a representation and warranty that the terms of the agreement 'are not inconsistent' with other contractual obligations. The changed text weakens this warranty to 'believed to be not inconsistent.' This introduces a contradiction because the original text provides a stronger assurance of consistency than the modified text. The contradicted text also contains the weakened warranty, but since the original text contained the strong version, the change to the weaker version introduces a contradiction that makes enforcement unclear and liability uncertain, as indicated in the Perturbation Explanation.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that the Receiving Party decides not to proceed with a Possible Transaction, the Receiving Party will promptly inform the Disclosing Party of that decision. In that case, or at any time upon the request of the Disclosing Party in its sole discretion and for any reason, the Receiving Party will as directed by the Disclosing Party promptly deliver, at the Receiving Party’s expense, to the Disclosing Party or destroy all Confidential Information furnished to the Receiving Party or the Receiving Party’s Representatives by or on behalf of the Disclosing Party pursuant hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that the Receiving Party decides not to proceed with a Possible Transaction, the Receiving Party **should consider** informing the Disclosing Party of that decision. In that case, or at any time upon the request of the Disclosing Party in its sole discretion and for any reason, the Receiving Party will as directed by the Disclosing Party promptly deliver, at the Receiving Party’s expense, to the Disclosing Party or destroy all Confidential Information furnished to the Receiving Party or the Receiving Party’s Representatives by or on behalf of the Disclosing Party pursuant hereto.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'will promptly inform' to 'should consider informing' weakens the obligation to notify the Disclosing Party of a decision not to proceed. This introduces an internal contradiction in Section 5 because the original 'will' indicates a mandatory action, while the modified 'should consider' suggests a discretionary action. This contradiction creates ambiguity about whether notification is required, thus impacting the Disclosing Party's ability to manage information and potentially pursue other transactions effectively.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 5. Return and Destruction of Confidential Information . In the event that the Receiving Party decides not to proceed with a Possible Transaction, the Receiving Party should consider informing the Disclosing Party of that decision. In that case, or at any time upon the request of the Disclosing Party in its sole discretion and for any reason, the Receiving Party will as directed by the Disclosing Party promptly deliver, at the Receiving Party’s expense, to the Disclosing Party or destroy all Confidential Information furnished to the Receiving Party or the Receiving Party’s Representatives by or on behalf of the Disclosing Party pursuant hereto. In the event of such a decision or request, all other Confidential Information prepared by the Receiving Party or on the Receiving Party’s behalf will be returned or destroyed and, upon the Disclosing Party’s request, the Receiving Party will provide the Disclosing Party with prompt written confirmation of the Receiving Party’s compliance with this paragraph; provided, however, that (a) if a legal proceeding has been instituted to seek disclosure of the Confidential Information, such material will not be destroyed until the proceeding is settled or a final judgment with respect thereto has been rendered; (b) the Receiving Party will not, in connection with the foregoing obligations, be required to identify or delete Confidential Information held electronically in archive or back-up systems in accordance with general systems archiving or backup policies and further provided that the Receiving Party will not, and the Receiving Party will use reasonable measures to cause its employees not to, access such Confidential Information so archived or backed-up; (c) the Receiving Party will not be obligated to return or destroy Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party to the extent the Receiving Party is required to retain a copy pursuant to applicable law; and (d) one copy of all Confidential Information may be kept by the Receiving Party’s outside counsel, if any, and such Confidential Information shall be fully protected by attorney-client privilege. Notwithstanding the return, destruction or continued possession pursuant to the terms of this Section 5 of the Confidential Information, the Receiving Party and the Receiving Party’s Representatives will continue to be bound by the Receiving Party’s obligations of confidentiality and other obligations and agreements hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'will promptly inform,' which is a mandatory action. The changed text alters this to 'should consider informing,' making the action discretionary. Section 5, the contradicted text, contains this changed language. Because the Receiving Party is given a choice in the changed and contradicted text, there is no longer a clear directive to inform the Disclosing Party, directly contradicting any expectation of mandatory notification implied elsewhere in the contract, which could impact the Disclosing Party's actions. This creates a strong in-text contradiction as per the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party irrevocably and unconditionally waives any objection that such Party may now or hereafter have to the laying of venue of any action, suit or proceeding arising out of this Agreement in the chancery courts of the State of Page 8 of 11 Delaware or, if the chancery courts of the State of Delaware lack jurisdiction, any Federal court located in the State of Delaware (and, in each case, any appellate court from any thereof), and further irrevocably and unconditionally waives and agrees not to plead or claim in any such court that any such action, suit or proceeding brought in any such court has been brought in an inconvenient forum.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party irrevocably and unconditionally waives any objection that such Party may now or hereafter have to the laying of venue of any action, suit or proceeding arising out of this Agreement in the chancery courts of the State of Page 8 of 11 Delaware or, if the chancery courts of the State of Delaware lack jurisdiction, any Federal court located in the State of Delaware (and, in each case, any appellate court from any thereof), and **may** further irrevocably and unconditionally waive and agrees not to plead or claim in any such court that any such action, suit or proceeding brought in any such court has been brought in an inconvenient forum.
perturbation.explanation: Modifying the venue clause in Section 11(h) from 'further irrevocably and unconditionally waives and agrees not to plead or claim' to 'may further irrevocably and unconditionally waive and agrees not to plead or claim' introduces an internal contradiction. The 'may' makes the waiver of objecting to the venue as an inconvenient forum optional, whereas the initial 'irrevocably and unconditionally waives' indicates a mandatory waiver. This creates ambiguity about whether a party can object to the venue, thereby undermining the enforceability of the venue selection clause and impacting potential legal proceedings.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 11. Miscellaneous . (h) Forum . Each Party irrevocably and unconditionally submits to the exclusive personal jurisdiction of the chancery courts of the State of Delaware or, if the chancery courts of the State of Delaware lack jurisdiction, any federal court located in the State of Delaware (and, in each case, any appellate court from any thereof), for any actions, suits or proceedings arising out of or relating to this Agreement (and each Party agrees not to commence any action, suit or proceeding relating thereto except in such courts, and further agrees that service of any process, summons, notice or document by U.S. registered mail to such Party’s address set forth above will be effective service of process for any action, suit or proceeding brought against such Party in any such court). Each Party irrevocably and unconditionally waives any objection that such Party may now or hereafter have to the laying of venue of any action, suit or proceeding arising out of this Agreement in the chancery courts of the State of Page 8 of 11 Delaware or, if the chancery courts of the State of Delaware lack jurisdiction, any Federal court located in the State of Delaware (and, in each case, any appellate court from any thereof), and may further irrevocably and unconditionally waive and agrees not to plead or claim in any such court that any such action, suit or proceeding brought in any such court has been brought in an inconvenient forum. (i) Waiver of Jury Trial . EACH OF THE PARTIES WAIVES TO THE FULLEST EXTENT PERMITTED BY APPLICABLE LAW ANY RIGHT IT MAY HAVE TO A TRIAL BY JURY WITH RESPECT TO ANY LITIGATION DIRECTLY OR INDIRECTLY ARISING OUT OF, UNDER OR IN CONNECTION WITH THIS AGREEMENT. Each of the Parties (A) certifies that no representative, agent or attorney of the other Party has represented, expressly or otherwise, that the other Party would not, in the event of litigation, seek to enforce the foregoing waiver and (B) acknowledges that it has been induced to enter into this Agreement by, among other things, the mutual waivers and certifications in this ARTICLE I Section 11(i) .
perturbation.justification: The original text states that each party 'irrevocably and unconditionally waives' any objection to venue. The changed text replaces this mandatory waiver with a discretionary one by using 'may further irrevocably and unconditionally waive'. This directly contradicts the initial 'irrevocably and unconditionally waives' as highlighted in the Perturbation Explanation, creating an ambiguity about whether a party can object to the venue as an inconvenient forum within Section 11(h) itself, which is also the Contradicted Text. This ambiguity is a strong in-text contradiction as it undermines the enforceability of the venue selection clause.
